gaps.gap_description: User-spec D6 mandates refactoring the Gallery anti-pattern 'Model.instance: Any?' into two new types: ModelDefinition (immutable) + ModelRuntimeHandle (mutable). Tech-spec never introduces ModelRuntimeHandle. The Shared Resources table explicitly shows that LlmModelInstance is still held on 'Model.instance'. The stale-instance guard ('compare by reference before close()') operates on the same Gallery anti-pattern field. ModelEntry wraps the existing Gallery Model class unchanged. The deviation from D6 is NOT documented in the User-Spec Deviations section.

AC-14 is the Phase 1 gate (TTFT ≤ 1.5× Gallery). Phase 1 APK TTFT is correctly instrumented via System.currentTimeMillis(). However, the Gallery baseline is measured by hand-stopwatch with no protocol: no minimum run count, no averaging, no logging of the baseline value, no tolerance for human reaction time error (~100-300ms). For a fast model (Gemma-4-E2B expected TTFT ~300-800ms), a single stopwatch measurement introduces 15-40% relative error — sufficient to make the 1.5× threshold ambiguous. Tech-spec has no tooling or structured protocol to make AC-14 objectively verifiable.

AC-15 (corrupted file → readable message + back button, not SIGSEGV) is listed as desired. Tech-spec Risk R5 mitigation references 'readable message via cleanUpMediapipeTaskErrorMessage'. However, the Architecture section and Task 9 description contain no explicit ChatScreen UI state for the error path (error message displayed + explicit back/retry navigation action). The error is surfaced as Result.failure to ChatViewModel, but the specific UI rendering of the error state with a back button is not specified anywhere in Architecture or Data Models. Partial coverage — error capture is described, error display UI is not.

overengineering.element: Decision T6: AppMainActivityModule — Hilt DI chain for MainActivity class injection
overengineering.reason: Decision T6 describes a 3-layer chain: AppMainActivityModule (@Module in :app) provides mainActivityClass: Class<*> → injected into DefaultDownloadRepository constructor → serialized as FQN string into WorkManager Data bundle → Class.forName(fqn) in DownloadWorker. The explicitly-rejected alternative (a) was hardcoding 'app.sanctum.machina.MainActivity' as a string constant in CoreRuntimeModule, which was rejected for 'module boundary' reasons. However, the module boundary argument does not hold: the FQN string is not a compile-time dependency of :core-runtime on :app — it is just a string value. For a Phase 1 spike with a single MainActivity class that will not change, the 3-layer DI chain adds 1 extra Hilt module and 1 constructor parameter to DefaultDownloadRepository for zero benefit over a BuildConfig string constant. The rejected alternative (a) was the simpler correct solution.

underengineering.reason: AC-9 requires PSS not exceeding 4 GB for Gemma-4-E2B. Tech-spec has no automated or agent-verifiable check for this (no TAC entry, no Agent Verification Plan step). Verification is entirely manual via 'adb shell dumpsys meminfo' — which is correct for on-device checks, but the Agent Verification Plan section does not list this check nor provide guidance on when during the test sequence it should be measured. Minor gap for a desired observable property of the system.

AllowlistLoaderTest checks that list.size == 2 from the fixture. Neither AllowlistLoader nor DefaultModelRegistry.init{} has a described guard for the case where the JSON parses successfully but returns 0 models (e.g., malformed models array). ModelManagerScreen would show an empty list silently — same failure mode that D8 justifies the test for. The unit test fixture is a copy of the production asset so this cannot occur in practice, but the boundary condition is undescribed in the spec.

deviations_integrity.detail: User-spec D6 is a named, explicit architectural decision mandating two new types. Tech-spec implements a different approach (ModelEntry + Mutex + stale-instance guard on Model.instance). The User-Spec Deviations section covers T1-T11 technical decisions and two extensions (ErrorLog refinement, serialization simplification) but has no entry for D6. Since D6 is not an infrastructure concern but a named architectural refactoring mandated by user-spec, the absence of an entry in User-Spec Deviations is a critical omission.

summary: 3 gaps (1 high / 2 medium), 1 overengineering (medium), 2 underengineering (low), 1 undocumented deviation (high). 13/16 ACs fully covered, 2 partial (AC-9, AC-14/AC-15), 1 missing (D6). Status: issues_found — 2 high-severity findings require resolution before approval.
